## Observed Failure Pattern
The `routing_audit_action.py` script creates tasks directly in kublai's queue when it detects issues. The task "Review routing audit findings and implement improvements" contains implementation directives ("implement it directly by editing the relevant file") but was assigned to kublai (router) instead of temujin (developer).

This violates architecture.md §4: "kublai never self-executes specialist work."

## Proposed Change to routing_audit_action.py

### Option A — Route via task_intake.py (preferred)

Instead of:
```python
task_file.write_text(f"

## Resolution
Pending. Awaiting Kurultai consensus vote.

---\nagent: kublai\n...")
```

Use:
```python
subprocess.run([
    "python3", str(SCRIPTS / "task_intake.py"),
    "--title", task_title,
    "--agent", "temujin",  # Implementation work goes to dev
    "--skill-hint", "/horde-implement"
], check=True)
```

### Option B — Add agent detection logic

If the audit task is:
- Pure analysis/reporting → jochi
- Implementation required → temujin
- Infrastructure issue → ogedei
